sources: CFPB Design & Testing Guidelines for Financial Products (2023) — https://www.consumerfinance.gov/data-research/research-reports/
WCAG 2.1 — Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, W3C — https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/
Nielsen Norman Group — Banking UX: Key Principles for Trust and Clarity (2022) — https://www.nngroup.com/articles/banking-ux/
Federal Reserve — 2023 Report on the Economic Well-Being of U.S. Households — https://www.federalreserve.gov/publications/2023-economic-well-being-of-us-households.htm
research.notes: Banking users are risk-averse and skeptical. Monospaced numerics prevent misreading amounts — a single transposed digit can result in financial loss or overdrafts. Audit trails and transaction IDs build institutional trust: users need to believe there is a ledger of record, not just an app. Every action must be reversible or clearly confirmable before execution; irreversible actions (wire transfers, account closure) require explicit multi-step confirmation. FDIC and regulatory compliance badges reduce anxiety in the first 5 seconds of account creation flows. Forms must be strictly linear — multi-column financial forms increase error rates (Baymard 2021). Error messages must be specific and actionable: 'Payment declined — insufficient funds. Available balance: $42.17' is correct; 'Something went wrong' is not. Users filing duplicate transfers due to unclear ACH feedback is a documented and costly failure mode. Novelty tolerance is low: users trust recognizable UI patterns (familiar card layouts, tabular statements) over novel interaction paradigms.
